rulings.Case Title: KATHY A. PIPPINS VS. ESTATE OF CHRISTINE EILEEN YOUNG, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Add A Party David Jah And Allow Time To Answer Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT DAVID JAH's Motion To Add A Party David Jah And Allow Time To Answer Complaint. The motion is denied. Although the motion is titled as a motion to add interested party as co-defendant, the motion is being considered as a motion to intervene. Mr. Jah has not shown that he is entitled to intervene under Code of Civil Procedure 387(a) or (b). In addition, the motion is untimely. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD WALSH VS. EXPLORATORIUM, A CALIFORNIA NON-PROFIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT EXPLORATORIUM's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied and the motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action for age discrimination. Demonstrating that plaintiff¿s replacement was significantly younger is not a required part of his prima facie case. Further, there is a triable issue of material fact whether Mr. Powell (a person in his mid-30¿s) replaced plaintiff. In addition, plaintiff has presented other evidence that demonstrates a discriminatory motive. (Walsh dec., par. 12). The motion is denied as to the intentional and negligent infliction of emotional distress causes of action (causes of action 3 and 4) because, for the reasons set forth above, defendant has not established that plaintiff cannot prove age discrimination. The Court grants summary adjudication on the second cause of action for wrongful discharge in violation of public policy. Plaintiff does not highlight a well-established public policy delineated in a statute, constitution, or regulation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The defendant is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party¿s counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: GEETA BHADAURIA VS. LUXOR CAB COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Deposition Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2013, Line 8, Motion To Compel Further Deposition Responses. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Judge Weixel as follows: Granted in part, and denied, in part. Mr. Alhanshali shall provide a signature exemplar created at deposition and the date, time and duration of his discussions with his counsel. The attorney-client privilege does not protect independent facts related to a communication. Mr. Alhanshali is not required to answer questions regarding: (1) his conversations with the person sent to the scene of the accident; and (2) the report that he testified about in his deposition because Mr. Alhanshali never saw the Luxor Cab report for after he wrote it. Having withdrawn his objections, Mr. Alhanshali shall respond to questions concerning his conference with counsel regarding the preparation of discovery responses. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MATS NAHLINDER VS. MARKMONITOR, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 9, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF MATS NAHLINDER's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ERNICE FAGAN VS. SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Request For Dimsissal Of Pltf'S Second Amended Complaint With Prejudice And Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion Of Request For Dimsissal Of Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint With Prejudice And Entry Of Judgment. Denied. When the sustained the demurrer with leave to amend on June 27, 2012, it was unaware that plaintiff had filed a voluntary dismissal on June 26, 2012 at 3:36 p.m., which was after the Court issued its tentative ruling sustaining the demurrer with leave to amend. Groth Bros. Oldsmobile, Inc. v. Gallagher (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 60 is distinguishable on its facts. The June 27, 2012 order sustaining the demurrer with leave to amend is vacated in light of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT BRENDA SPANN's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The motion is continued to January 16, 2013, on the Court's motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ERNICE FAGAN VS. SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 9, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The motion is continued to be heard on February 11, 2013. The opposing party shall provide a courtesy copy of the opposition/response with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date by January 14, 2013. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The Court has copies of the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/MJM)
